chunks.text:
Restaurant: Ooink
Cuisine: Japanese-Chinese Fusion Ramen
Philosophy: Respect the ingredients. All fresh ingredients sourced daily. Zero-waste approach - free extra noodles available by the register.
Story: Named 'Ooink' with a pig logo. The restaurant celebrates bold fusion flavors that break traditional ramen conventions. Started in Capitol Hill in 2016 above a QFC store, expanded to Fremont in 2022.

Location: Capitol Hill (Original Location)
Address: 1416 Harvard Ave, Seattle, WA 98122
Phone: (206) 568-7669
Hours: {'sunday_thursday': '11:30 AM - 9:00 PM', 'friday_saturday': '11:30 AM - 11:00 PM'}

Location: Fremont
Address: 3630 Stone Way N, Seattle, WA 98103
Phone: (206) 588-2035
Hours: {'sunday_thursday': '5:00 PM - 9:30 PM', 'friday_saturday': '5:00 PM - 11:00 PM'}

Category: Signature Ramen Bowls
Name: Mala Kotteri Ramen
Price: $20
Description: Ooink's most iconic dish. Rich, creamy, silky pork broth with intense garlic flavor, mala spice (Sichuan numbing and spicy sensation), topped with char siu, bamboo shoots, aji-tamago, scallion, black garlic oil, and crispy garlic chips. The kotteri (thick-style) broth clings perfectly to the fresh thin noodles.
Flavor: Savory, garlicky, mala spicy-numbing, rich, complex, creamy
Spice level: mild - extra hot (adjustable 1-4 scale)
Dietary options: []
Allergens: ['eggs', 'soy', 'wheat', 'pork', 'garlic', 'sesame']
Best for: Garlic lovers, spice enthusiasts, anyone seeking bold complex flavors
Reviews: Favorite ramen in Seattle, Reminds of mala hotpot broth, Perfect noodle texture - chewy and bouncy, Love the garlic oil and crispy chips, Complex flavor stays with you long after, Best bowl in Seattle for garlic lovers, Intense but balanced flavors

Category: Signature Ramen Bowls
Name: Mala Ramen
Price: $19.50
Description: Spicy, numbing ramen with our signature mala flavor profile - Sichuan peppercorn and ground chili heat. Silky pork broth with mala oil and tare topped with char siu, bamboo shoots, aji-tamago, scallion. Slightly less garlic-forward than Mala Kotteri but same mala sensation.
Flavor: Spicy, numbing (mala), savory, slightly lighter than kotteri
Spice level: Medium-hot (adjustable 1-4 scale)
Dietary options: []
Allergens: ['eggs', 'soy', 'wheat', 'pork']
Best for: Those wanting mala spice without the extra garlic intensity
Reviews: Perfect mala flavor balance, Broth is addictive, Great for mala lovers on a budget

Category: Signature Ramen Bowls
Name: Kotteri Ramen
Also known as: cot terry ramen cottage ramen koh terry ramen thick ramen creamy ramen
Price: $19.50
Description: Rich, thick-style (kotteri) ramen for garlic lovers. Creamy pork broth infused with black garlic oil, topped with pork belly char siu, bamboo shoots, aji-tamago, scallion, and garlic chips. No spice - pure rich garlic flavor.
Flavor: Savory, intensely garlicky, creamy, rich, no heat
Spice level: None - not spicy
Dietary options: []
Allergens: ['eggs', 'soy', 'wheat', 'pork', 'garlic', 'sesame']
Best for: Garlic enthusiasts, those avoiding spice, comfort food seekers, non-spicy options
Reviews: Rich and flavorful, Best for garlic lovers, Creamy broth perfectly coats noodles, Perfect for those who want flavor without heat

Category: Signature Ramen Bowls
Name: Shoyu Ramen
Also known as: soy sauce ramen, show you ramen, show yu ramen, soy ramen
Price: $18.50
Description: Traditional soy sauce-based broth ramen. Light dashi broth with house shoyu tare and burnt green onion. Simple yet soul-satisfying with house-made noodles. Topped with char siu, bamboo shoots, aji-tamago, scallion, and aroma oil.
Flavor: Savory, umami-rich, light, clean, subtle
Spice level: None
Dietary options: []
Allergens: ['eggs', 'soy', 'wheat']
Best for: Traditionalists, those seeking lighter broth, non-spicy options, and those who want flavor without heat
Reviews: Soul satisfying, Clean broth with depth, Perfect for those wanting flavor without spice or richness

Category: Signature Ramen Bowls
Name: Shio Ramen
Also known as: shio ramen she oh ramen salt ramen shiro ramen
Price: $18.50
Description: Salt-based (shio) ramen with yuzu citrus brightness. Very light dashi broth with house shio tare, toasted wheat bran noodles, char siu, bamboo shoots, aji-tamago, scallion, and aroma oil. Citrus notes make this refreshing.
Flavor: Savory, bright citrus (yuzu), light, clean, slightly tangy
Spice level: None
Dietary options: []
Allergens: ['eggs', 'soy', 'wheat']
Best for: Those wanting lighter broth, citrus lovers, nice contrast to spicy dishes
Reviews: Refreshing and balanced, Yuzu adds nice brightness

Category: Signature Ramen Bowls
Name: Mapo Tofu Ramen
Price: $19.50
Description: Fusion mala ramen with Sichuan twist. Silky pork soup with shoyu flavor, ground pork, silky tofu, spicy mala heat, topped with noodles, zacai (Sichuan pickled mustard green), char siu, and scallion. Spice level adjustable.
Flavor: Mala spice, savory, numbing, Sichuan-inspired
Spice level: Medium-hot (adjustable 1-4)
Dietary options: []
Allergens: ['eggs', 'soy', 'wheat', 'pork']
Best for: Sichuan cuisine lovers, those wanting tofu in their ramen

Category: Signature Ramen Bowls
Name: Spicy Vegetarian Miso Ramen
Price: $19.50
Description: Miso-based vegetarian broth topped with green vegetables (Bok choy), corn, corn-butter, 2 slices of tofu skin, shredded mushrooms, seaweed, sesame sos, house famous garlic peanut chilli-oil and fresh noodles. Completely plant-based when made without butter. Warming miso with numbing spicy heat.
Flavor: Miso, mala spice, earthy mushrooms, complex
Spice level: Medium
Dietary options: ['vegetarian', 'can_be_made_vegan']
Allergens: ['soy', 'wheat', 'possible nuts in some ingredients', 'contains nuts']
Best for: Vegetarians, vegans (ask about vegan version), miso lovers seeking spice,
Reviews: Great vegetarian option, Substantial and filling, Spice balances miso well, Veggies are Bok choy

Category: Signature Ramen Bowls
Name: Vegetarian Miso Ramen
Price: $18.50
Description: Miso-flavored broth with topped with vegetables, mushrooms, corn, corn-butter, tofu skin, bamboo shoots, seaweed and scallion.
Flavor: Miso umami
Spice level: No-spicy
Dietary options: []
Allergens: ['soy', 'wheat', 'diary']
Best for: Miso lovers wanting kick, vegetarians, vegan options

Category: Signature Ramen Bowls
Name: Shanghai Style Ramen (No Pork)
Price: $12.50
Description: Nontraditional Shanghai-inspired broth without pork. Clean, savory water broth in chicken fat oil topped with scallion, and burnt onions. Lighter but still flavorful.
Flavor: Savory, clean, umami
Spice level: None
Dietary options: ['']
Allergens: ['soy', 'wheat']
Best for: Those avoiding pork, seeking lighter broth, kid friendly

Category: Appetizers
Name: Malaysian Fried Chicken (Spicy Ayam Goreng)
Price: $13
Description: Chef Ooi's Malaysian heritage showcased in this standout dish. Crispy-fried chicken marinated with fresh chili, galangal, lemongrass, turmeric, garlic, and curry leaves. Perfectly balanced - crispy exterior with moist, flavorful interior. Addictive Malaysian spice profile.
Flavor: Savory, spicy, aromatic, perfectly balanced crispy-juicy
Spice level: Medium-hot
Allergens: ['soy', 'wheat', 'garlic']
Best for: Those seeking bold spiced fried chicken, Malaysian food lovers
Reviews: Best fried chicken in Seattle, Perfect crispy-moist balance, Addictive numbing spice, Malaysian flavors incredibly balanced, Absolutely worth the price, Frequently sells out during peak hours

Category: Appetizers
Name: Spicy Crispy Chicken Sandwich
Price: $13
Description: Spicy fried chicken sandwich. Crispy fried chicken patty served on bread with house spice blend. Some say it's some of the best in Seattle. Frequently sells out.
Flavor: Spicy, crispy, savory
Spice level: Medium-hot
Allergens: ['wheat', 'soy']
Best for: Those wanting handheld spicy chicken
Reviews: Some of the best in Seattle, Frequently sells out, Perfect spice balance

Category: Appetizers
Name: Gyoza (pork-dumplings)
Also known as: goza go za joza dumplings potstickers pot stickers pork dumplings
Price: $8
Description: Pan-fried dumplings with pork and fresh ingredients. Ooink gyoza are famous for generous filling and perfect golden-brown exterior. Hand-rolled with care. Traditionally 6 pieces per order.
Flavor: Savory, umami-rich, perfectly crispy bottom
Allergens: ['wheat', 'soy', 'pork']
Best for: Dumpling lovers, perfect starter
Reviews: Best gyoza in city, Amazing flavor, Tender meat, crispy bottoms, Generous filling, Incredibly fresh

Category: Appetizers
Name: Cucumber Salad
Price: $8
Description: Crisp Persian cucumber combined with crushed peanuts, garlic, and shallot, tossed together with sesame oil. Refreshing, crunchy, with sesame-forward dressing. Beautifully presented.
Flavor: Crispy, nutty, sesame, spicy, refreshing
Spice level: Light to medium
Allergens: ['peanuts', 'sesame', 'garlic']
Best for: Those seeking refreshing contrast, vegetable lovers
Reviews: Refreshing start, Sesame-forward flavor, Beautifully presented

Category: Sides & Extras
Name: Extra Toppings
Price: $3.50-7.50
Description: Additional toppings available to customize your ramen. Common extras include char siu (pork), extra egg (aji-tamago), garlic chips, bamboo shoots, green onion, tofu skin, and vegetables.

Category: Sides & Extras
Name: Extra Noodles
Price: $4.00
Description: Ooink provides complimentary extra noodles available by the register as part of their zero-waste philosophy. Self-serve - grab a packet as you leave.

Category: Sides & Extras
Name: Mala Chili Oil
Price: $2.50
Description: Ooink's signature spicy chili oil with Sichuan numbing spice. Intensely flavorful - start with a little and add more. Used to power their mala dishes. Also sold in retail bottles for home use.
Flavor: Fiery heat, numbing Sichuan spice, balanced, addictive
Reviews: Perfectly balanced flavor and heat, Extremely addictive, Makes any bland cooking taste amazing, Intense so start small, 10/10 would recommend

Category: Sides & Extras
Name: Garlic Peanut Chili Oil
Price: $2.50
Description: Ooink's signature spicy garlic peanut chili oil with Sichuan numbing spice. Intensely flavorful - start with a little and add more. Also sold in retail jars for home use.
Flavor: balanced, addictive, mildly spicy
Reviews: Perfectly balanced flavor and heat, Extremely addictive, Makes any bland cooking taste amazing, Intense so start small, 10/10 would recommend, goes well with everything

Question: What should I order if I'm new to Ooink?
Answer: Start with the Mala Kotteri Ramen - it's our signature dish and best represents what we do. Pair it with the Malaysian Fried Chicken appetizer (Ayam Goreng) for the full Ooink experience. If you can't handle spice, go with Kotteri Ramen instead. Both showcase our fresh noodles and flavor complexity.

metadata.question: What should I order if I'm new to Ooink?

chunks.text:
Question: What is 'shoyu ramen'?
Answer: Traditional soy sauce-based flavour made with house shoyu tare and burnt green onion, and anchovy powder

metadata.question: What is 'shoyu ramen'?

chunks.text:
Question: What is the difference between 'shio' and 'shoyu'?
Answer: shio ramen is more salty than shoyu

metadata.question: What is the difference between 'shio' and 'shoyu'?

chunks.text:
Question: What does 'mala' mean?
Answer: Mala is a Sichuan Chinese flavor profile combining two sensations: 'ma' (numbing) from Sichuan peppercorns and 'la' (spicy heat) from chili. You get both a numbing sensation and spicy burn simultaneously - it's addictive and complex. Ooink's mala uses this authentic profile with shoyu flavor.

metadata.question: What does 'mala' mean?

chunks.text:
Question: What's the difference between Mala and Mala Kotteri?
Answer: Both have our signature mala (spicy, numbing) flavor. Mala Kotteri is creamier with a thick broth base and lots MORE garlic flavor - it comes with black garlic oil and crispy garlic chips on top. Mala Kotteri is richer and more intense. Regular Mala is slightly lighter with the same mala sensation but less garlic emphasis. Mala Kotteri is our most popular dish.

metadata.question: What's the difference between Mala and Mala Kotteri?

chunks.text:
Question: What does 'kotteri' mean?
Answer: Kotteri means a thick, rich, creamy style of ramen broth in Japanese. Our kotteri broths are infused with garlic or mala spices and cling beautifully to the noodles. These are comfort-food ramen for those who want maximum flavor intensity.

metadata.question: What does 'kotteri' mean?

chunks.text:
Question: Is Ooink spicy? Can I adjust the spice level?
Answer: Many Ooink dishes feature mala-forward (spicy, numbing) flavors, but you have FULL control. Our ramen with adjustable heat comes with a 1-4 spice scale. Level 1 is mild, level 4 is hot. We also have non-spicy options like Kotteri Ramen (pure garlic), Shoyu Ramen (traditional soy), and Shio Ramen (citrus salt). Ask our staff and we'll help you find the right heat level.

metadata.question: Is Ooink spicy? Can I adjust the spice level?

chunks.text:
Question: What's your best non-spicy option?
Answer: Try Kotteri Ramen - rich, creamy, intensely garlicky, ZERO spice. Perfect for garlic lovers. Or try Shoyu Ramen (traditional soy sauce broth) for something cleaner and lighter. Both showcase our fresh noodles without heat.

metadata.question: What's your best non-spicy option?

chunks.text:
Question: Do you have vegetarian or vegan options?
Answer: Yes! We offer Spicy Vegetarian Miso Ramen, and Shanghai Style Ramen (no pork) can be made vegetarian. Ask about making these vegan - we can skip the egg and butter. We also have vegetarian appetizers like Spicy Cucumber Salad and Kabucha Squash with Miso & Curry.

metadata.question: Do you have vegetarian or vegan options?

chunks.text:
Question: Do you have gluten-free options?
Answer: Currently, we do not offer gluten-free noodles. Our noodles are made fresh daily from wheat flour. We cannot guarantee zero cross-contamination due to our kitchen setup. Please inform our staff of any allergies.

metadata.question: Do you have gluten-free options?

chunks.text:
Question: What allergens should I know about?
Answer: Our menu contains common allergens including wheat (noodles, soy sauce), soy (soy sauce, miso, tofu), eggs (in aji-tamago toppings), and various proteins (pork, chicken). Many dishes contain garlic and sesame. Some dishes contain tree nuts (peanuts, pecans). Always inform our staff of allergies - we'll help you find safe options and cannot guarantee zero cross-contamination.

metadata.question: What allergens should I know about?

chunks.text:
Question: How long is the wait time and when are you open?
Answer: Fremont location (3630 Stone Way N): Sun-Thu 5:00 PM-9:30 PM, Fri-Sat 5:00 PM-11:00 PM. Food prep typically takes 5-10 minutes once you order at our kiosk. During peak dinner hours (5:30-8:30 PM on weekends), there may be 10-30 minute wait for seating. Best tip: Arrive after 6:30 PM on busy evenings or call (206) 588-2035.

metadata.question: How long is the wait time and when are you open?

chunks.text:
Question: How do I order at Fremont?
Answer: Self-service kiosk system: 1) Walk in and add your name to waitlist if needed, 2) When a table is ready, order at the digital kiosk (tablet interface), 3) Pay at kiosk with card or cash, 4) Seat yourself. Food is brought to your table when ready. It's fast and efficient. Do not forget to buss the dishes

metadata.question: How do I order at Fremont?

chunks.text:
Question: Can I customize my ramen?
Answer: Absolutely! You can adjust spice levels (1-4 scale), noodle firmness, and add extra toppings like char siu, soft boiled eggs, garlic chips, vegetables for small additional charge. We want you to enjoy exactly what you want.

metadata.question: Can I customize my ramen?

chunks.text:
Question: What makes Ooink special compared to other ramen shops?
Answer: Three things: 1) Fresh noodles made daily from scratch - our thin, bouncy noodles are praised as the best texture in Seattle. 2) Bold, nontraditional fusion flavors - mala-forward broths and Malaysian-inspired dishes show Chef Ooi's unique approach. 3) Ingredient quality and care - we source fresh daily and have zero-waste philosophy. Customers say we're their favorite ramen place in Seattle.

metadata.question: What makes Ooink special compared to other ramen shops?

chunks.text:
Question: Do your fried chicken dishes sell out?
Answer: Yes, Malaysian Fried Chicken (Spicy Ayam Goreng) and Spicy Crispy Chicken Sandwich are very popular and frequently sell out during peak hours (5:30-8:30 PM on weekends). We recommend arriving early or calling ahead at (206) 588-2035 to check availability.

metadata.question: Do your fried chicken dishes sell out?

chunks.text:
Question: Is there outdoor seating at Fremont?
Answer: Yes! Fremont location has a small outdoor patio area, perfect for nice weather. Seating also available inside at tables and the bar near the kitchen.

metadata.question: Is there outdoor seating at Fremont?

chunks.text:
Question: What's the parking situation at Fremont?
Answer: Street parking only - no dedicated lot. Parking can be challenging during peak evening hours (5:30-8:30 PM weekends). Plan 10-20 minutes to find a spot on busy nights. Best strategy: arrive after 6:30 PM on weekends for easier parking and fewer seating waits.

metadata.question: What's the parking situation at Fremont?

chunks.text:
Question: Do you have delivery or takeout?
Answer: Yes! Order online through ooinkramen.com, DoorDash, or Uber Eats for delivery or pickup. Fremont hours for takeout: Sun-Thu 5:00 PM-9:30 PM, Fri-Sat 5:00 PM-11:00 PM. You can also order retail ramen kits online from shopooink.com.

metadata.question: Do you have delivery or takeout?

chunks.text:
Question: What's your payment policy and is tipping included?
Answer: We accept credit cards, Samsung Pay, and Apple Pay. Service charge is included in your final bill - transparent pricing. Additional tipping is available if you'd like.

metadata.question: What's your payment policy and is tipping included?

chunks.text:
Question: What's unique about Fremont location vs Capitol Hill?
Answer: Capitol Hill (original) is tiny, intimate, lunch-dinner location - great for solo/couples. Fremont opened in 2022, dinner-only (5 PM opening), larger space better for groups, has outdoor patio, full bar, digital kiosk ordering. Fremont is more buzzy and group-friendly while Capitol Hill is quiet and cozy.

metadata.question: What's unique about Fremont location vs Capitol Hill?

chunks.text:
Question: Tell me about the owners and Ooink's story.
Answer: Ooink is owned by Chef Chong Boon Ooi and his wife Jiaxin Wang. Chef Ooi brings Malaysian culinary roots visible in signature dishes like Ayam Goreng (Malaysian fried chicken). They opened the original Capitol Hill location in 2016 above a QFC store. They expanded to Fremont in 2022. The restaurant is named 'Ooink' with a pig logo, celebrating bold fusion flavors that break ramen conventions. Their philosophy: respect the ingredients, source fresh daily, zero waste (free noodles by register).

metadata.question: Tell me about the owners and Ooink's story.

chunks.text:
Question: What do locals order vs what do tourists order?
Answer: Locals order the Mala Kotteri Ramen (our signature) and come back repeatedly. They also know to grab the Malaysian Fried Chicken before it sells out. Tourists often ask for recommendations and we guide them to start with Mala Kotteri or non-spicy Kotteri depending on heat tolerance. Both groups praise our gyoza - it's beloved by everyone.

metadata.question: What do locals order vs what do tourists order?

chunks.text:
Question: How long does food typically take?
Answer: Fresh ramen typically takes 10-15 minutes from order to table. Appetizers like fried chicken and gyoza take 5-10 minutes. Our made-to-order approach ensures quality but means slight waits. During peak hours, total experience (seating + food) may take 45 minutes to 1 hour.

metadata.question: How long does food typically take?

chunks.text:
Question: Is Ooink kid friendly?
Answer: Yes, Ooink is family-friendly. We have vegetarian and non-spicy options for kids. The casual, efficient service model works well for families. Note that Fremont location opens at 5 PM (dinner only), so good for family dinner.

metadata.question: Is Ooink kid friendly?

chunks.text:
Question: what's kid friendly ramen?
Answer: Shanghai style ramen (no pork) is kid friendly with just scallion and burnt onions on top of the ramen with chicken flavoured water broth and is adjustable upon user request

metadata.question: what's kid friendly ramen?

chunks.text:
Question: Is there WiFi?
Answer: No, WiFi is not available for customers.

metadata.question: Is there WiFi?

chunks.text:
Question: Do you have bathrooms?
Answer: Yes, Fremont location has one restroom facility available for customers.

metadata.question: Do you have bathrooms?

chunks.text:
Question: What about noise level and atmosphere?
Answer: Fremont is a buzzy, upbeat, social atmosphere - more like a vibrant bar scene. Capitol Hill is quieter and more intimate. Both are casual, fast-paced, and fun. Fremont's open kitchen and patio create lively energy.

metadata.question: What about noise level and atmosphere?

chunks.text:
Question: What should I order if I have a light appetite?
Answer: Try Very Light Dashi Ramen or Shio Ramen - both are lighter broths. Pair with Spicy Cucumber Salad for a refreshing, lighter meal. These highlight our broth quality and fresh noodles without being heavy.

metadata.question: What should I order if I have a light appetite?

chunks.text:
Question: What should I order if I'm VERY hungry?
Answer: Get Mala Kotteri Ramen (rich, filling) with Malaysian Fried Chicken and Gyoza. The creamy kotteri broth and protein-rich toppings plus the substantial appetizers will definitely satisfy. Many customers pair ramen with 2-3 appetizers.

metadata.question: What should I order if I'm VERY hungry?

chunks.text:
Question: What dishes pair well together?
Answer: Great combinations: (1) Mala Kotteri Ramen + Malaysian Fried Chicken + Spicy Cucumber Salad = bold spice + refreshing contrast. (2) Kotteri Ramen + Gyoza = rich garlic broth + crispy dumplings = classic pairing. (3) Shoyu Ramen + Kabucha Squash = light broth + earthy vegetable = balanced meal. (4) Any ramen + Malaysian Fried Chicken = always works, chicken is universally loved.

metadata.question: What dishes pair well together?

chunks.text:
Question: What do reviews say about Ooink?
Answer: Ooink averages 4.4-4.8 stars. Customers consistently praise: 'best noodle texture in Seattle,' 'favorite ramen place in Seattle,' 'Malaysian Fried Chicken is some of the best in Seattle,' 'gyoza are incredible,' 'fast service,' 'bold complex flavors,' 'fresh ingredients visible in every bite.' Main considerations: prices are $17-20+ per ramen (reflects quality), Fremont parking can be challenging, popular items sell out.

metadata.question: What do reviews say about Ooink?

chunks.text:
Question: Do you guys do reservations?
Answer: Unfortunately we do not do reservations at neither of our locations, it's first come first serve!

metadata.question: Do you guys do reservations?
